volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 1.3.325b - Section 1.3.363d)

sections.label: Section 1.3.325b
sections.original: Quia enim non venalem iám habeo Phoenicium .
sections.translation: Why , because I ’ ve got no Phœnicium to sell .

sections.label: Section 1.3.326
sections.translation: Not got her ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.326b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , not I , indeed .

sections.label: Section 1.3.326c
sections.translation: Pseudolus , go fetch the sacrifice , the victims , the sacrificers [*] , that I may make offering to this supreme Jove . For this Jupiter is now much more mighty to me than is Jupiter himself .

sections.label: Section 1.3.329
sections.original: Nolo victumas : agninis me extis placari volo .
sections.translation: I want no victims ; with the entrails of minae [*] I wish to be appeased .

sections.label: Section 1.3.330
sections.original: Propera , quid stas ? ei accerse agnos . audin quid ait Iuppiter ?
sections.translation: Make haste . Why do you hesitate ? Go fetch the lambs ; do you hear what Jupiter says ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.331
sections.original: Iam hic ero ; verum extra portam mi etiam currendumst prius .
sections.translation: I ’ ll be here this moment ; but first I must run as far as beyond the gate [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.3.332
sections.translation: Why thither ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.332b
sections.original: Lanios inde accersam duo cum tintinnabulis ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll fetch two sacrificers thence , with their bells ; at the same time I ’ ll fetch thence two bundles of elm twigs , that this day a sufficiency may be provided for the sacrifice to this Jove .

sections.label: Section 1.3.335
sections.translation: Away to utter perdition [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.3.335b
sections.translation: Thither shall the pimping Jupiter go .

sections.label: Section 1.3.336b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.336c
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; because , i ’ faith , so long as I shall be alive , you ’ ll never be a man well to do .

sections.label: Section 1.3.338
sections.original: Ex tua re non est , ut ego emoriar .
sections.translation: It isn ’ t for your interest that I should die .

sections.label: Section 1.3.338b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.338c
sections.translation: This way ; because , if I ’ m dead , there will be no one worse than yourself in Athens . For your interest ( to CALIDORUS ) it is that I should die .

sections.label: Section 1.3.340
sections.original: Dic mihi , obsecro hercle , verum serio hoc quod te rogo .
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , in serious truth , tell me this that I ask you — have you not got my mistress , Phœnicium , on sale ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.342
sections.original: Non edepol habeo profecto , nam iam pridem vendidi .
sections.translation: By my faith , I really have not ; for I ’ ve now sold her already .

sections.label: Section 1.3.343
sections.translation: In what way ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.343b
sections.translation: Without her trappings , with all her inwards [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.3.344
sections.translation: What ? Have you sold my mistress ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.345
sections.translation: For twenty minae ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.345b
sections.original: Vtrum vis , vel quater quinis minis ,
sections.translation: Or , in other words , for four times five minae , whichever you please , to a Macedonian Captain ; and I ’ ve already got fifteen of the minae at home .

sections.label: Section 1.3.347
sections.translation: What is it that I hear of you ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.347b
sections.translation: That your mistress has been turned into money .

sections.label: Section 1.3.348
sections.original: Cur id ausu ’ (s e) s facere ?
sections.translation: Why did you dare to do so ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.348b
sections.translation: ’ Twas my pleasure ; she was my own .

sections.label: Section 1.3.348c
sections.translation: Hallo ! Pseudolus . Run , fetch me a sword .

sections.label: Section 1.3.349b
sections.translation: What need is there of a sword ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.349c
sections.translation: With which to kill this fellow this instant , and then myself .

sections.label: Section 1.3.350
sections.original: Quin tu ted occidis potius ? nam hunc fames iam occiderit .
sections.translation: But why not kill yourself only rather ? For famine will soon be killing him .

sections.label: Section 1.3.351
sections.original: Quid ais , quantum terra † tegit hominum periurissume ?
sections.translation: What do you say , most perjured of men as many as are living upon the earth ? Did you not take an oath that you would sell her to no person besides myself ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.353
sections.translation: I confess it .

sections.label: Section 1.3.353b
sections.translation: In solemn form [*] , to wit .

sections.label: Section 1.3.353c
sections.translation: Aye , and well considered too .

sections.label: Section 1.3.354
sections.translation: You have proved perjured , you villain .

sections.label: Section 1.3.354b
sections.translation: I sacked the money at home , however .

sections.label: Section 1.3.355
sections.original: ego scelestus nunc argentum promere possum domo :
sections.translation: Villain as I am , I am now able to draw upon a stock of silver in my house ; whereas you who are so dutiful , and born of that grand family , haven ’ t a single coin .

sections.label: Section 1.3.357
sections.original: Pseudole , adsiste altrim secus atque onera hunc maledictis .
sections.translation: Pseudolus , stand by him on the other side and load this fellow with imprecations .

sections.label: Section 1.3.357b
sections.translation: Very well . Never would I run to the Praetor [*] with equal speed that I might be made free .

sections.label: Section 1.3.359
sections.translation: Heap on him a multitude of curses .

sections.label: Section 1.3.359b
sections.original: Iam ego te differam dictis meis .
sections.translation: Now will I publish you with my rebukes .

sections.label: Section 1.3.360
sections.translation: Thou lackshame !

sections.label: Section 1.3.360b
sections.translation: ’ Tis the fact .

sections.label: Section 1.3.360c
sections.translation: Villain !

sections.label: Section 1.3.360d
sections.translation: You say the truth .

sections.label: Section 1.3.360e
sections.translation: Whipping-post !

sections.label: Section 1.3.361
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.361b
sections.translation: Robber of tombs !

sections.label: Section 1.3.361c
sections.translation: No doubt .

sections.label: Section 1.3.361d
sections.translation: Gallows-bird !

sections.label: Section 1.3.361e
sections.translation: Very well done .

sections.label: Section 1.3.362
sections.translation: Cheater of your friends !

sections.label: Section 1.3.362b
sections.translation: That ’ s in my way .

sections.label: Section 1.3.362c
sections.translation: Parricide !

sections.label: Section 1.3.362d
sections.translation: Proceed , you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.363
sections.translation: Committer of sacrilege !

sections.label: Section 1.3.363b
sections.translation: I own it .

sections.label: Section 1.3.363c
sections.translation: Perjurer !

sections.label: Section 1.3.363d
sections.translation: You ’ re telling nothing new [*] .
